title: Loan Condition Continuation | As of December 31, 2026
content:
Charge-Offs & Recoveries
The year-to-date loss-and-recovery picture reflects a significant contraction in gross charge-offs across the portfolio. Year-to-date gross charge-offs totaled $911 thousand for the period ended March 31, 2026.
New Vehicle loans and Used Vehicle loans were identified as the primary sources of losses during this reporting period, with New Vehicle accounting for $336 thousand in charges and Used Vehicle contributing $307 thousand. All Other Unsecured segments also contributed to total charge-offs at a level of $57 thousand.
Recovery data was not disclosed in the source materials for this period.
The credit team will continue its focus on collections efforts and portfolio monitoring as loss activity remains elevated compared to prior periods.

Allowance & CECL Position
The allowance balance declined by 0.7% quarter-over-quarter, settling at $2985 thousand (presented as a positive reserve figure of $2985 thousand) for the period ended December 31, 2026. This reduction occurred alongside year-to-date gross charge-offs totaling $911 thousand and loan balance trends that have moderated in recent quarters.
Reserve levels address:
- Risk concentrations within New Vehicle and Used Vehicle portfolios
- Current delinquency and charge-off trends
- Expected future credit conditions and portfolio performance
Management continues to evaluate reserve adequacy against emerging risk factors.

Overall Assessment
The overall credit risk level for the period is characterized by elevated but declining loss activity. Year-to-date gross charge-offs of $911 thousand occurred alongside a CECL allowance balance that declined slightly quarter-over-quarter, reflecting adjustments in expected future losses. Capital and reserve coverage remains sufficient to absorb current net charges while maintaining prudent liquidity buffers. Management maintains its ongoing focus on underwriting standards and portfolio monitoring.
